aggregate_function.description: Aggregate function to apply to the selected values before applying the condition. [none, sum, average, max, min]

aggregate_window.description: The time window to aggregate the selected values. It follows the format of a number followed by a time unit

alert.description: If true, the monitor is in alert state.

condition_type.description: A comparison operation to apply to the value selected by the source_selector [greater_than, greater_than_or_equal_to, less_than, less_than_or_equal_to, equal_to, not_equal_to]

disabled.description: If true, the monitor will not be evaluated.

last_routed_at.description: The last time the monitor was evaluated and routed.

per_device.description: Only relevant when using an aggregate_function. If true, the monitor will be evaluated per device, | rather than across the set of selected devices. If true then if a single device matches the specified criteria, | and alert will be created, otherwise the aggregate function will be applied across all devices.

routing_cooldown_period.description: The time period to wait before routing another event after the monitor | has been triggered. It follows the format of a number followed by a time unit.

silenced.description: If true, alerts will be created, but no notifications will be sent.

source_selector.description: A valid JSONata expression that selects the value to monitor from the source. | It should return a single, numeric value.

source_type.description: The type of source to monitor. Supported values are "event" and "heartbeat".

threshold.description: The type of condition to apply to the value selected by the source_selector

usage_scope.description: For usage monitors: the scope of aggregation. Supported values are "device" and "fleet".

usage_type.description: For usage monitors: the type of data usage to monitor. Supported values are "cellular" and "satellite".

usage_window.description: For usage monitors: the rolling time window in days to sum usage over (e.g. 30 for 30 days).
